name: Vugu
description: A Vue-inspired library for building web UIs in Go + WebAssembly with single-file components

description.value: A Vue-inspired library for building web UIs in Go + WebAssembly with single-file components

initial-release.comment: Repository created March 2019; first public release v0.2.1 in April 2020

active-maintenance.comment: v0.4.0 released May 2024 with mage-based build refactor; 609 commits total

reactivity-model.comment: Modification tracking via vugu:"data" struct tags to avoid unnecessary re-renders

component-model.comment: Single-file components (.vugu files) with HTML template and Go code sections; compiles to Go structs

rendering-paradigm.comment: Declarative HTML templates with Vue-like directives (vg-if, vg-for, vg-content)

state-management-approach.comment: Built-in state via Go struct fields with vugu:"data" tags; wire function pattern for dependency injection

opinionatedness.comment: Library philosophy - you control program flow, wiring, and render loop; Vugu provides utilities

backward-compatibility.comment: Not 1.0 yet; experimental with breaking changes between versions (e.g., v0.1.0 to v0.2.0 refactor)

primary-language.comment: Written in Go (93%); JavaScript only for minimal WASM interop layer

compiles-to.comment: Compiles Go to WebAssembly; supports both standard Go and TinyGo compilers

typescript-support.comment: Go framework; uses Go's type system instead of TypeScript

template-syntax.comment: .vugu files with Vue-like HTML templates; vg-if, vg-for, vg-content directives; Go expressions in attributes

styling-approach.comment: CSS included directly in .vugu component files; scoped to component

build-required.comment: Requires Go toolchain with GOOS=js GOARCH=wasm; vgrun development server or manual go generate

wasm-interop.comment: DOM access via embedded JS file; Go wasm_exec.js for runtime support; event.Prop() for JS data access

reactive-primitives.comment: Struct fields tagged with vugu:"data" are tracked for modifications and trigger re-renders

computed-values.comment: Compute(ctx) lifecycle method called before each render for derived state

two-way-binding.comment: vgform package provides two-way binding for form controls via StringPtr, SliceOptions, etc.

event-handling.comment: Declarative @event syntax (e.g., @click='c.HandleClick()'); vugu.DOMEvent provides event data

list-rendering.comment: vg-for directive for iteration over slices and maps

component-slots.comment: Slots via DefaultSlot and named slots (<vg-slot name="...">); implemented as Go anonymous functions

suspense.comment: No built-in suspense; async operations handled via goroutines with EventEnv.Lock()

context-provide-inject.comment: Wire function pattern provides dependency injection; components receive dependencies via setter interfaces

refs-direct-dom-access.comment: Direct DOM access possible via js package; vugu/js provides DOM manipulation utilities

official-router.comment: vgrouter is the official first-party URL router for Vugu

nested-routes.comment: Routes can be registered sequentially; partial path matching allows nested route patterns

dynamic-routes.comment: BindParams feature for route parameter binding (documentation notes it needs more docs)

route-guards.comment: No documented route guard/middleware system; routes are matched in registration order

lazy-loading-routes.comment: No lazy loading; WASM binary compiled as single unit

scroll-restoration.comment: Not documented; ListenForPopState handles browser history but no scroll restoration mentioned

built-in-state.comment: Component state via Go struct fields with modification tracking via vugu:"data" tag

built-in-global-state.comment: Wire function pattern for injecting shared state objects across components

global-state-library.comment: No external state library needed; wire function pattern is built-in solution

devtools-state-inspection.comment: No browser devtools for state inspection; debugging via standard Go/WASM tools

state-persistence.comment: No built-in persistence; can use browser storage APIs manually

official-cli.comment: vgrun CLI for development server; vugugen for code generation; vugufmt for formatting

hot-module-replacement.comment: vgrun devserver provides file watching and automatic page reload on .vugu file changes

browser-devtools.comment: No Vugu-specific browser extension; relies on standard WebAssembly debugging

vs-code-extension.comment: vscode-vugu extension provides syntax highlighting for .vugu files

error-messages.comment: Go compiler errors are helpful; type safety catches many errors at compile time

documentation-quality.comment: Adequate docs with getting started guide, API concepts, and FAQ; some sections marked as needing more documentation

interactive-tutorial.comment: No interactive tutorial; getting started guide is text-based

playground.comment: No online playground due to Go/WASM compilation requirements

bundle-size-core.comment: Standard Go compiler produces multi-megabyte WASM; ~2MB+ compressed typical for basic apps

bundle-size-full.comment: TinyGo compilation reduces to hundreds of kilobytes; ~500KB typical with TinyGo and -no-debug flag

virtual-dom.comment: Not Virtual DOM; uses modification tracking and direct DOM synchronization

compile-time-optimization.comment: Go compiler optimizations; TinyGo provides additional size optimization for WASM output

tree-shaking.comment: Go compiler eliminates unused code; TinyGo more aggressive for WASM targets

code-splitting.comment: No WASM binary splitting; entire app compiles to single WASM file

lazy-components.comment: No lazy component loading; all components part of single WASM binary

ssr-capable.comment: StaticHTMLEnv generates static HTML on server; ParserGo/ParserGoPkg for server-side rendering

streaming-ssr.comment: Renders to static string; no streaming SSR support

partial-hydration.comment: No documented hydration; SSR generates static HTML without client-side interactivity takeover

js-framework-benchmark.comment: Not included in js-framework-benchmark; Go/WASM framework with limited benchmark data

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: Go module, not npm package; distributed via Go modules (go get)

component-libraries.comment: No known UI component libraries; ecosystem is small

meta-framework.comment: No meta-framework; can be paired with any Go HTTP server

major-companies-using.comment: No publicly documented production usage by major companies; experimental project

official-sponsor-backer.comment: Community-driven project by Brad Peabody and contributors; no corporate backing

discord-community-size.comment: Uses Slack (#vugu channel in GolangBridge workspace) instead of Discord

stack-overflow-questions.comment: Very few Stack Overflow questions; community primarily uses GitHub issues and Slack

job-market-demand.comment: Niche; experimental Go/WASM framework with minimal job market presence

official-testing-library.comment: StaticHTMLEnv can be used for testing by generating static output for comparison

testing-library-name.comment: StaticHTMLEnv renders components to static HTML for testing; mage manages test process

component-testing.comment: Components can be tested via static renderer; standard Go testing with wasm targets

e2e-testing-integration.comment: No documented E2E testing integration; standard browser testing tools can be used

snapshot-testing.comment: StaticHTMLEnv output can be used for snapshot comparison testing

mobile-framework.comment: No official mobile framework; mobile browser support not well tested

pwa-support.comment: No built-in PWA support; would require manual service worker configuration

desktop-framework.comment: No official desktop framework; could theoretically work with Electron or Tauri

learning-curve.comment: Moderate; requires Go knowledge but Vue-like templates are familiar; simpler than Rust alternatives

concepts-to-learn.comment: Go basics, .vugu files, vg-directives, component struct, wire function, event handling, WASM build

time-to-hello-world.comment: ~5 minutes with Go installed; vgrun -new-from-example creates project, vgrun devserver.go starts it

migration-path.comment: No migration guides from JS frameworks; requires rewrite in Go

incremental-adoption.comment: WASM output can be integrated into existing websites; doesn't require full rewrite
